title: What the ''Boys'' Did Over There
author: Henry L. Fox
description: Personal accounts and recollections of soldiers coping with body lice, poisonous gas, rats, and death in the trenches during WWI. - Summary by Jeffery Smith

title: Selections from Battle-Pieces and Aspects of the War
author: Herman Melville
description: Published in 1866, Battle-Pieces and Aspects of the War is a collection of poems about the Civil War by Herman Melville. Many of the poems are inspired by second- and third-hand accounts from print news sources (especially the Rebellion Record) and from family and friends. A handful of trips Melville took before, during, and after the war provide additional angles of vision into the battles, the personalities, and the moods of war. In an opening note, Melville describes his project not so much as a systematic chronicle (though many of the individual poems refer to specific events) but as a kind of memory piece of national experience. The “aspects” to which he refers in the title are as diverse as “the moods of involuntary meditation—moods variable, and at times widely at variance.” Much of the verse is stylistically conventional (more so than modern readers perhaps expect from the author of Moby-Dick), but the shifting subjectivities and unresolved traumas that unfold in the collection merit repeated contemplation. Melville’s Battle-Pieces do not offer a neatly versified narrative of the Civil War but rather kaleidoscopic glimpses of shifting emotions and ambivalent reflections of post-war America.(Professor Meredith Neuman)

title: War Surgery - From Firing Line to Base
author: Henry Stanley Banks, Basil Hughes
description: One of the first volumes dedicated to systematized medical treatment of soldiers in modern warfare, including a chapter on specific care for airmen, by British doctors who served on the front lines of WWI. Graphic descriptions (and images in the source book) of war wounds are not for the weak of heart. - Summary by BellonaTimes

title: America's Part
author: Henry J. Reilly
description: "What part did we take in the Great War? Did we only lend money to our Allies, or did we really fight?" Henry J. Reilly, the author of "America's Part", who was both a Brigadier General and a newspaper correspondent, addressed European cynicism about American participation in World War 1 in this 1928 book. Even ten years after the war's end, the U.S. role in it remained a subject of heated debate. Our allies resented America for contributing too little and too late to the war effort, yet Americans benefited far more than they from the peace. Reilly found that European histories of the war overlooked American forces, failing to discuss their military contribution. "America's Part" was intended to fill this gap in the literature and inform both Americans and Europeans of the true story. Reilly details the goals of American commanders, the challenges they faced, and the pivotal role U.S. forces played in the war's ultimate outcome. - Summary by Ted Lienhart

title: Tales Of The Trail; Short Stories Of Western Life
author: Henry Inman
description: This 1898 collection of thirteen previously published articles exhibits the acute perception of one of the most popular writers of the late 19th-early 20th centuries. “These "Tales of the Trail" are based upon actual facts which came under the personal observation of the author… and will form another interesting series of stories of that era of great adventures, when the country west of the Missouri was unknown except to the trappers, hunters, and army officers.” Henry Inman (1837 – 1899) was an American soldier, frontiersman, and author. He served in the military during the Indian campaigns and the American Civil War, having earned distinction for gallantry on the battlefield. He was commissioned lieutenant general during the Indian wars. He settled in Kansas and worked as a journalist and author of short stories and books of the plains and western frontier. - Summary by Wikipedia, Book Preface, David Wales

title: Recollections of Rifleman Harris
author: Henry Curling, Benjamin Randell Harris
description: The recollections of a British infantryman who served in the British army during the Napoleonic Wars. (Summary by Graham Keeling)

title: State of the Union Addresses by United States Presidents (1893 - 1896)
author: Grover Cleveland
description: The State of the Union address is a speech presented by the President of the United States to a joint session of the United States Congress, typically delivered annually. The address not only reports on the condition of the nation but also allows the President to outline his legislative agenda (for which he needs the cooperation of Congress) and national priorities. This album contains recordings of addresses from Grover Cleveland's second term. - Summary by Wikipedia

title: War Memories of an Army Chaplain
author: Henry C. Trumbull
description: Henry Clay Trumbull was the Chaplain of the Tenth Regiment of Connecticut Volunteers in the American Civil War. In this memoir, he gives his observations and recollections of his experiences, including time spent as a prisoner of war. He describes the life and duties of an Army chaplain, as well as gives his thoughts on slavery, emancipation, and its aftermath. (Summary by northstar95)

title: Fighting The Boche Underground
author: Harry Davis Trounce
description: Harry Trounce was an American mining engineer of British birth and parentage who joined the British Army in October 1915. After the briefest of training in England and France he joined the 181st Tunnelling Company at Silly-sur-la-Lys in Flanders in January 1916. This book is a detailed blow-by-blow (in every sense of the words) account of life underground as a junior officer in this company. - Summary by Paul Hampton

title: Full Speed Ahead: Tales From The Log Of A Correspondent
author: Henry Beston
description: “These tales are memories of several months spent as a special correspondent attached to the forces of the American Navy on foreign service…. [I have] been content to chronicle the interesting incidents of the daily life as well as the achievements and heroisms of the friends who keep the highways of the sea…. I would not end without a word of thanks to the enlisted men for their unfailing good will and ever courteous behaviour.” Henry Beston (1888-1968) was an American author. In 1918, Beston became a press representative for the U.S. Navy. Highlights from this period include being the only American correspondent to travel with the British Grand Fleet and to be aboard an American destroyer during combat engagement and sinking during World War I. This 1919 book describes these experiences. Lists of names have been omitted from the Preface. - Summary by Book Preface, Wikipedia, David Wales

title: Bloody Beaches: The Marines at Peleliu
author: Gordon D. Gayle
description: On D-Day 15 September 1944, five infantry battalions of the 1st Marine Division’s 1st, 5th, and 7th Marines, in amphibian tractors (LVTs) lumbered across 600–800 yards of coral reef fringing smoking, reportedly smashed Peleliu in the Palau Island group and toward five selected landing beaches. That westward anchor of the 1,000-mile-long Caroline archipelago was viewed by some U.S. planners as obstacles, or threats, to continued advances against Japan’s Pacific empire. The Marines in the LVTs had been told that their commanding general, Major General William H. Rupertus, believed that the operation would be tough, but quick, in large part because of the devastating quantity and quality of naval gunfire and dive bombing scheduled to precede their assault landing. On some minds were the grim images of their sister 2d Marine Division’s bloody assault across the reefs at Tarawa, many months earlier. But 1st Division Marines, peering over the gunwales of their landing craft saw an awesome scene of blasting and churning earth along the shore. Smoke, dust, and the geysers caused by exploding bombs and large-caliber naval shells gave optimists some hope that the defenders would become casualties from such preparatory fires; at worst, they would be too stunned to respond quickly and effectively to the hundreds of on-rushing Marines about to land in their midst. - Summary by Gordon D. Gale

title: First From the Front
author: Harold Ashton
description: In this brief book I have lifted a very small corner of the curtain of war, to tell of my adventures — a week in the North Sea, and a breathless score of days in Northern France. I have touched upon both Tragedy and Comedy as they came my way. The tragedy is terrible enough - I have put it down plainly and unvarnished. From Tragedy to Comedy, it is but a step, along the gloomiest corridor of life one sees the flash of the cap and hears the rattle of the bells. Otherwise, it would be unbearable. And if my little bell would seem to jangle out of tune, I cannot help it. The bell was there; now and again it rang, and some of us smiled at the music of it. (Extract from the Introduction of First From The Front) At the outbreak of the First World War, Harold Ashton became War Correspondent of The Daily News and almost immediately managed to join up with a Danish Sea captain who had been commissioned to transport food supplies from Denmark to various ports in England. After this North Sea adventure, he then made his way to Northern France and accompanied various military units, both British and French as they deployed into various sectors of the war zone. His reporting focused on the 'human interest' aspect of the events that he witnessed, and he provided his readers with a unique insight into many aspects of the military buildup, as he chronicled the early stages of what was eventually to be called, The Great War. - Summary by Steve C

title: Birth of Yugoslavia, Volume 2
author: Henry Baerlein
description: Henry Baerlein expands on his articles first published in the Fortnightly Review to explain the historical context and challenges facing Yugoslavia, a multi-ethnic country located in southeastern Europe that was formed immediately after World War I as the Kingdom of Serbs, Croats, and Slovenes – and which disintegrated in 1992 and is now in present-day (2024) parts of Bosnia and Herzegovina, Croatia, Republic of Kosovo, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Serbia, and Slovenia. (Summary by Keith Marohn)

title: Memories of the Civil War
author: Henry B. James
description: Henry B James enlisted in the Union Army at the age of 20, and fought in many of the key battles of the Civil War. Thirty years later, he wrote this short memoir, which he begins by saying,, "Mine was not an exceptional experience, only that of many a boy of ’61, but it may partly answer the question so often asked: 'What did the privates do?'" - Summary by Ciufi Galeazzi

title: Western Boundary, and Other Essays
author: Helmuth Karl Bernhard Graf von Moltke
description: Moltke’s The Western Boundary was originally published in the journal Deutsche Vierteljahrsschrift in 1841. The essay came in the wake of calls the previous year by Adolphe Thiers—at the time Prime Minister and Foreign Minister of France—to annex the left bank of the Rhine in ‘satisfaction’ after France’s capitulation to Britain and Russia over the ‘Oriental question’. Helmuth von Moltke here examines Franco-German relations from the fall of the Roman Empire to undercut narratives then emanating from France. Holland and Belgium in their Mutual Relations was published in a brochure format in 1830. It concerns the situation in Belgium kicked off by the French Revolution of that year. Considerations in the Choice of Railway Routes from 1843 is an early study of railways through a technical and economic set of lenses. Railways would go on to be a vital cornerstone of German mobilization and strategic planning—and an important component of his own military thought. Moltke first became interested in railways in 1841 when the Berlin–Hamburg railway extended him the offer of a seat on the board of directors. He left the board in 1844 but would continue to dwell on the subject for decades to come. - Summary by Alister

title: A Century of Dishonor
author: Helen Hunt Jackson
description: In this work, a predecessor to "Ramona", HH describes the systematic process of segregation, dispossession, and relocation that Native Americans experienced from the late XVIII to the late XIX century. When written, the last conflicts between Native Americans and the US Government were being held. She wrote the book in efforts to fight for the rights of the Natives and to change people's opinions and governmental policies. The Appendix has been left out. - Summary by Mario Pineda

title: Sea-Power in the Pacific
author: Hector Charles Bywater
description: During the three decades preceding the Pacific War, the United States placed no small amount of effort in gaming out what a such a conflict against Japan might look like. Ultimately, the Pacific War looked very little like the crystallization of these efforts expressed in War Plan Orange. The Americans and the Japanese were both heavily influenced by the theories of sea power and decisive battle of Alfred Thayer Mahan, despite the lacklustre absence of any such climactic decision at sea in the Great War. The Pacific War would bear even less resemblance to Naval War College preconceptions as battleships were sidelined by the carrier—initially out of necessity, though as planes improved, and the doctrine for their employment were clarified, the superior capability they provided to shape the battlespace would soon become as clear as daylight. Sea-Power in the Pacific, published in 1921, is military writer Hector Charles Bywater’s prediction which in some respects – together with his 1925 novel, The Great Pacific War – hits far closer to the mark than any actual naval war planning of the interwar period. The impact of these two books in the United States is questionable, but – according to a 1970 article by journalist William H. Honan from American Heritage – both books were translated into Japanese and circulated among naval officers during the interwar period. Pictured on the cover is Yamashita Gentarō who was chief of the Navy General Staff at the time of this book’s publication. He was a member of the Fleet Faction opposed to the Washington Naval Treaty which was the hot coal of contention in the fight for supremacy between the Naval General Staff and the Navy Ministry. It was also the Navy General Staff which very quickly organised and circulated a translation of Sea-Power in the Pacific upon its publication. To what extent this book influenced Japanese military thinking is uncertain, but it clearly did exert an influence on the critical political struggles at play within the Imperial Navy in the 1920s. - Summary by Alister

title: Over Here and Over There
author: Harry Zody
description: In publishing this book I have no intention whatsoever to offer a work of great literary value. As such it would undoubtedly be a failure, because, being of a non-English-speaking race, and only having been in this country a comparatively short time before going over to France, I cannot claim a mastery of the English language. It has merely been my intention to express the spirit which led me to America and thence with Pershing's Expeditionary Forces to France. (From Introductory)

title: Mobilizing Woman-Power
author: Harriot Stanton Blatch
description: Harriot Stanton Blatch, a suffragist in her own right, was the daughter of Elizabeth Cady Stanton who was one of the champions of women’s rights and a force in the suffragist movement. In this volume, Blatch focuses on the mobilization of women during World War I, both in Europe and American. These courageous women stepped into roles formerly the domain of men and provided the essential services crucial to the success of the outcome of the war. - Summary by Larry Wilson

title: Little Wars
author: H. G. Wells
description: A Game for Boys from twelve years of age to one hundred and fifty and for that more intelligent sort of girl who likes boys' games and books. With an Appendix on Kriegspiel Miniature wargaming got its start with the publication in 1913 of this thoroughly entertaining little account of how H.G. Wells, with certain of his friends, took their childhood toys and turned play into acceptable middle-aged sport by subjecting the exercise to the civilizing influence of actual rules.While wargaming progressed far past these beginnings, Wells observes how "little wars" with even his elementary rules can suggest the wholesale crudity of the real thing."You have only to play at Little Wars three or four times to realise just what a blundering thing Great War must be. Great War is at present, I am convinced, not only the most expensive game in the universe, but it is a game out of all proportion. Not only are the masses of men and material and suffering and inconvenience too monstrously big for reason, but--the available heads we have for it, are too small. That, I think, is the most pacific realisation conceivable, and Little War brings you to it as nothing else but Great War can do."Wells leaves almost hanging the tantalizing concept that we might someday simulate war, as an instrument of international decision-making, rather than practice actual combat. But most of this book is just the fun of evicting the boys from the playroom and spending happy days there, away from the "skirt-swishers", developing the framework under which two gentlemen might meet and accumulate boastable victories! (Summary by Mark F. Smith)

title: With the Anzacs in Cairo
author: Guy Thornton
description: Guy Thornton recounts his experiences serving as a military chaplain with the Australian and New Zealand Army Corps (ANZAC) while stationed in Egypt during World War I. His writing is opinionated, emotionally vulnerable, and written in very colorful, vivid prose. He recounts both his successes and failures as a pastor. Of particular concern to Thornton, are his soldiers’ interactions with the Wasa'a- the red-light district of Cairo which was popular with the soldiers who often patronized prostitutes. Thornton also expresses his experiences exploring Cairo, his work as a chaplain, and the various social services catering to soldiers. - Summary by Adam Bielka

title: Martyrdom Of Belgium
author: Gérard Cooreman
description: The title says it all. World War I narratives of German activities in Belgium after the German invasion of this neutral country. - Summary by david wales

title: Chronicles of America Volume 12 - Washington and his Comrades in Arms
author: George Wrong
description: This twelfth volume in the Chronicles of America (series) follows the lengthy and difficult war against England for independence as led by George Washington.

title: Designing Experiences That Leave a Lasting Impression
author: Dervon Hamprton
description: Designing Experiences That Leave a Lasting Impression follows the work of experiential designer Dervon Hampton, exploring how interior design, brand strategy, and project leadership come together to create immersive trade show experiences. Featuring the Harley-Davidson 2026 Washington, D.C. Auto Show as a case study.

title: Piece by Piece: Building Better Businesses
author: Paula Allen
description: Hosted by Paula Allen, this show breaks down what it really takes to build a sustainable, scalable business—without the overwhelm. Each episode explores the essential “pieces” of growth, from strategy and systems to mindset, marketing, and leadership.

title: Escape of a Princess Pat
author: George Pearson
description: Being the full account of the capture and fifteen months' imprisonment of Corporal Edwards, of the Princess Patricia's Canadian Light Infantry, and his final escape from Germany into Holland. (Summary from Book subtitle)This is to celebrate Remembrance Day 2008. NB. There are recordings of Chapter 4 by two different readers. The zip file includes both. Variety is the spice of life!

title: Soldier Of The Legion; An Englishman's Adventures Under The French Flag in Algeria And Tonquin
author: George Manington
description: An educated gentleman, Mr Manington has given an insight into the unusual experiences of an Englishman in the French Foreign Legion, such as no ordinary "mercenary" could have done. Most of the narrative deals with Tonquin, and the fighting there against the rebels in their forest fastnesses. Incidentally, in giving an account of his friendship for the native sergeant, Doy-Tho, the author has been able to impart to the pages of the book an Oriental atmosphere that we think will prove attractive to the reader. - Summary by Editors' Note

title: Stonewall Jackson and the American Civil War
author: George Francis Robert Henderson
description: Stonewall Jackson and the American Civil War is a book combining a biography and military history of Confederate Lt. General Thomas Jonathan "Stonewall" Jackson's actions and results during the American Civil War. Written by British soldier and author G.F.R. Henderson, it was originally published in 1898 and became the author's most well-known work. A link to maps of the Civil War battles discussed in this book: https://centurypast.org/centur...

title: Rebel's Recollections
author: George Eggleston
description: George Cary Eggleston's Civil War memoir begins with a separate essay on the living conditions and political opinions of Virginia’s citizenry before secession. The body of the work contains vivid descriptions and accounts of the men and women of the South during the time of the Confederacy. Eggleston praises its war heroes, Robert E. Lee, Stonewall Jackson, and Jeb Stuart, but is highly critical of Jefferson Davis and of his government’s inefficiencies, red-tape, and favoritism. The book concludes with the war's end and a tribute to the character of the newly freed slaves. This informative and engaging work, much of which appeared in The Atlantic Monthly, enjoyed great popularity throughout the country. Originally published in 1874, it went through four editions by 1905. ( Lee Smalley)

title: McClellan's Own Story
author: George Brinton McClellan
description: Memoirs of General George Brinton McClellan, commanding general of the Army of the Potomac during the early years of the American Civil War. The work covers the time that McClellan commanded the Army of the Potomac, including the Peninsula Campaign, the Seven Days' Battles, and the Battle of Antietam.

title: Narrative of the Expedition to Dongola and Sennaar
author: George Bethune English
description: As a second lieutenant in the United States Marine Corps during the War of 1812 assigned to Marine Corps headquarters, English sailed to the Mediterranean, and was among the first citizens of the United States known to have visited Egypt. Shortly after arriving in Egypt he resigned his commission, converted to Islam and joined Isma'il Pasha in an expedition up the Nile River against Sennar in 1820, winning distinction as an officer of artillery. He published his Narrative of the Expedition to Dongola and Sennaar (London 1822) regarding his exploits. (Introduction adapted by obform from Wikipedia)

title: Angels of the Battlefield
author: George Barton
description: "Angels of the Battlefield: A History of the Labors of the Catholic Sisterhoods in the Late Civil War" chronicles the compassionate services of these dedicated women during the bitter and bloody U.S. Civil War. These accounts also offer some important historical details, giving some important insights into the people and events of the war. This is the Second Edition, Revised and Enlarged. - Summary by Larry Wilson

title: Rough Talks by a Padre
author: Geoffrey Studdert Kennedy
description: Geoffrey Anketell Studdert Kennedy MC was an ordained Anglican priest, who in the early stages of World War I volunteered for duty as a chaplain to the British army on the Western Front. Studdert Kennedy was one of the most well-known and highly respected chaplains of the First World War, who though himself unarmed, accompanied the British troops into battle at the front line to provide whatever help he could to lessen their suffering and to raise their morale. He was soon widely known by his nom de guerre ‘Woodbine Willie’. This nickname was acquired from his practice of handing out (at his own expense) cigarettes along with any spiritual comfort required or maybe just a friendly word as he shared experiences with the men in the trenches. In 1917, he was awarded the Military Cross (one of the military’s highest awards) for “showing conspicuous gallantry, devotion to duty and the greatest courage and disregard for his own safety in attending to the wounded under heavy fire” during action at Messines Ridge having selflessly entered no-man's land to help the wounded of both sides during an attack on the German frontline. This book, Rough Talks of a Padre, was derived from a series of lectures delivered by Studdert Kennedy to officers and men in almost every part of France. And as the author notes in the book’s foreword: ‘They are lectures, not essays, and are therefore often loose and colloquial in style’ and are based on his personal reflections of reconciling his requirement to provide moral, spiritual and pastoral leadership to the troops in his ‘parish’ with the necessary day to day survival of them all as a fighting military unit. (Summary by Steve C)

title: Commentaries on the Gallic War
author: Gaius Julius Caesar
description: Commentarii de Bello Gallico (English: Commentaries on the Gallic War) is Julius Caesar's firsthand account of the Gallic Wars, written as a third-person narrative. In it Caesar describes the battles and intrigues that took place in the nine years he spent fighting local armies in Gaul that opposed Roman domination.The work has been a mainstay in the teaching of Latin to schoolchildren, its simple, direct prose lending itself to that purpose. It begins with the frequently quoted phrase "Gallia est omnis divisa in partes tres", sometimes quoted as "Omnia Gallia in tres partes divisa est", meaning "All Gaul is divided into three parts". (Summary from Wikipedia)

title: Four Weeks in the Trenches
author: Fritz Kreisler
description: A brief record of the fighting on the Eastern front in the great war by a participant in that great conflict (Summary by FNH)

title: History of the Thirty Years War, Volume 4
author: Friedrich Schiller
description: Volume Four covers the battle of Lutzen (November, 1633) to the death of Wallenstein (February, 1634) focusing on the reaction of the Protestant forces following the death of Gustavus Adolphus at Lutzen, and Wallenstein's actions leading to his death. - Summary by Alan Winterrowd

title: Regulations for the order and discipline of the troops of the United States : part I
author: Friedrich Wilhelm Ludolf Gerhard Augustin, Baron von Steuben
description: More commonly know as "The Blue Book" written by "Baron von Steuben" this publication was key to organizing the young American military in the Revolutionary War with England. This book served both as a regulation and a how-to manual. Each officer was required to: purchase a copy upon being commissioned, carry it at all times, read it, and use it. (Summary by David Olson) A Librivox recording of The Life of Frederick William Von Steuben: Major General in the Revolutionary Army

title: Life of Frederick William von Steuben
author: Friedrich Kapp
description: The Life of Frederick William Von Steuben: Major General in the Revolutionary Army - tells the story of Baron Steuben, who had been an officer in the Prussian army. Considered one of the fathers of the United States Army, he had a leading role in improving the Continental Army during the American Revolution and turning them into a professional fighting force. See text for Appendix I Pedigree of Baron Steuben and well as many other charts regarding musters of the Revolutionary War. (Summary by progressingamerica)

title: Hospital Transports
author: Frederick Law Olmsted
description: In the American Civil War, The United States Sanitary Commission, staffed by volunteers, may be viewed as a precursor to The Red Cross. It supplemented the medical care of the armed services medical corps. Its doctors, nurses, administrators, go-fers, money, and supplies saved thousands of lives, providing medical care to the wounded and solace to the families of the dead. This memoir of one campaign gives a flavor of the challenges faced, frustrations endured, and medical battles lost and won. Explanatory note: “contraband” refers to a black slave, esp. a fugitive or captured slave. Summary by david wales and Oxford English Dictionary.

title: Aeroplanes and Dirigibles of War
author: Frederick A. Talbot
description: "Aeroplanes and Dirigibles of War" is an interesting read of the beginnings of air warfare in World War I. Anyone interested in early aviation and armament will find this a fascinating work. By William Tomcho.

title: Captain Called It Mutiny
author: Frederic Van de Water
description: On December 1, 1842, three crew members of the U.S. Navy brig Somers were hanged for mutiny. The alleged leader was eighteen-year-old Philip Spencer, son of President Tyler's Secretary of War. The author, a distant relative of one of the crew members suspected in the incident, attempts to uncover the facts of this controversial moment in United States naval history. - Summary by Mark Harrington

title: Stories of the Victoria Cross
author: Frank Mundell
description: The Victoria Cross is the highest military award that can be made to members of the British and Commonwealth armed forces for acts of gallantry and valour “in the face of the enemy”. The medal was instituted by Royal Warrant in 1856 by Queen Victoria, who had taken an active interest in the reports and despatches during the battles of the Crimean War; especially the details describing the exceptional bravery and gallantry of individual soldiers of all ranks whilst under intense conflict. This book, Stories of the Victoria Cross was one in a series of inspirational texts and ‘heroic writings’ by the Victorian author Frank Mundell and published by The Sunday School Union. The stories presented here relate to actual events that occurred in the field of battle around the turn of the last century and the extraordinary efforts of individuals or groups of soldiers when facing life-threatening situations whilst under fire. - Summary by Steve C

title: War the Creator
author: Frank Gelett Burgess
description: Gelett Burgess, an American writer, penned this gripping account of the profound change that war caused in a young Frenchman he knew. “Because he was my friend, because he was so lovable, because he suffered much, I want to try to tell the story of a boy who, in two months, became a man. I happened to see him first just before the war began, and not again until after he had been wounded; and the change in him was then so great that I could not rest until I had learned how it had been brought about.” – From War the Creator (Lee Smalley)

title: Fourteen Months in American Bastiles
author: Francis Key Howard
description: Francis Key Howard recounts in this book his life as a political prisoner of the United States. He points out that he was held captive at the same location where his grandfather was inspired to write the national anthem about the "land of the free," which makes a very stunning contrast. The sufferings that were imposed on him by the Union forces had the effect of solidifying his determination to resist unjust governmental dictates. (Introduction by Katie Riley)

title: Shout Treason: the Trial of Aaron Burr
author: Francis F. Beirne
description: Much has been written about Aaron Burr, Thomas Jefferson, John Marshall and other founding fathers who were at the center of United States v. Burr in which the former Vice President was tried for treason. Comparatively little has been written about the conduct of the trial. This book concerns itself with that landmark, precedent setting case. - Summary by Brian Fullen

title: Army Letters from an Officer's Wife, 1871-1888
author: Frances M. A. Roe
description: "There appeared from the bushes in front of me, and right in the path, two immense gray wolves . . . Rollo saw them and stopped instantly, giving deep sighs, preparing to snort, I knew . . . To give myself courage, I talked to the horse, slowly turning him around . . . when out of the bushes in front of us, there came a third wolf! The situation was not pleasant and without stopping to think, I said ‘Rollo, we must run him down - now do your best’ and taking a firm hold of the bridle, and bracing myself in the saddle, I struck the horse with my whip and gave an awful scream. . .” Thus, the spunky and resourceful Frances Roe recounts one of her many adventures. Frances was the young wife of a West Point Army officer, whose career took them both to frontier garrisons in what are now the states of Kansas, Colorado, Oklahoma, Utah, Montana, and Wyoming. Her letters home to her family in upstate New York, written between 1871 and 1888, and published in book form in 1909, are a fascinating chronicle of life on the frontier. Despite the grittiness of keeping house in tent-and-log-cabin quarters, Frances took to Western life, learning to shoot and ride (side saddle), fly fish for trout and hunt buffalo. Her letters, chatty and detailed, open windows on varied aspects of frontier and army life: army protocol (including the right of senior officers to bump subordinates out of their housing ); the Indian tribes--Arapahoe, Cheyenne, Apache, Kiowa (ceremonial visits from chiefs as well as Indian warfare); the black cavalry troops (at Camp Supply in Indian Territory); Chinese cooks (also Polish, Irish, and enlisted men doing kitchen duty). Her letters span years of rapid change in the West. They touch on the disappearance of the buffalo herds, the decline of the Indian tribes and the coming of the railroads. Even Yellowstone Park, established in 1872, gets a mention: “Now that the park can be reached by railroad, all of the generals, congressmen, and judges are seized with a desire to inspect it--in other words, it gives them a fine excuse for an outing at Uncle Sam’s expense.”Her emotions run deep when she writes: “I love army life here in the West, and I love all the things that it brings to me--the grand mountains, the plains, and the fine hunting.” But, Frances’ husband, Fayette Washington Roe, is eventually promoted to a headquarters desk job. Frances tries to evinse enthusiasm for town life: “since I cannot return to the plains, I might as well go to the city, where we will meet people of culture.” But even shopping for the necessary clothes distresses her: “Parasols have bothered me. I would forget to open them in the street, and would invariably leave them in the stores when shopping and then have to go about looking them up.” She then utters a timeless cry of urban angst: “It is the feeling of loneliness I mind here--of being lost and no one to search for me.” (written at a hotel in Omaha, Nebraska in August 1888) Anyone with nostalgia for or curiosity about the Old American West will certainly enjoy this book. (Summary by Sue Anderson)

title: English Woman-Sergeant in the Serbian Army
author: Flora Sandes
description: Flora Sandes was the only British woman officially to serve as a soldier in World War I. Initially a St. John Ambulance volunteer, she traveled to Serbia, where, in the confusion of war, she was formally enrolled in the Serbian army. While the Serbian Army was regrouping in Corfu, Ms. Sandes returned to England to recuperate and publish these memoirs.(Summary from Wikipedia)

title: Needs of Europe, Its Economic Reconstruction
author: Fight the Famine Council
description: In October 1920, the International Economic Conference in London gathered invitees to review post-war Europe's ravaged economies. Speakers included economists and representatives from various European aid and reconstruction groups. The talks offered a mix: some presented data on production and inflation, while others detailed the widespread misery of disease, famine, and poverty. The conference aimed to identify areas for relief or economic stimulation. The book's modern value lies in its depiction of the enormous economic and social devastation caused by four years of war. - Summary by Ted Lienhart

title: Sword of Deborah
author: F. Tennyson Jesse
description: "The Sword of Deborah" contains the reflections of a woman journalist visiting women working behind the lines in France during World War I. She writes: "I was glad to have seen all the different convoys I had, because no two had been to me alike, and to each I am indebted for a differing expression of the same vision, which is the vision splendid of a duty undertaken gladly and sustained with courage. From my first convoys -- the Fannies and the G.S.V.A.D.'s -- I got the wonderful facts of it, at the V.A.D. Convoy at E---- I caught that side of it which I as most glad of all to encounter, and at the V.A.D. Convoy at T----- I found that delightful spirit of sheer joy in danger which is too precious to be allowed to die out of the world just because there happens to be, at present, such a great deal too much danger let loose upon it." (Summary by MaryAnn)

title: Young Folks' History of the American Revolution
author: Everett T. Tomlinson
description: This work has grown out of the desire frequently expressed to the writer when he has been lecturing on the American Revolution, that in some way the experiences of the people as well as the deeds of the armies in that trying period might be presented to the present generation. The author has given to the public this narrative, in which the course of the armies in the various campaigns is followed and, at the same time, many of the forgotten or ignored experiences and deeds of the common people are also incorporated. (Summary by the author)

title: World’s Story Volume IX: England
author: Eva March Tappan
description: This is the ninth volume of the 15-volume series of The World’s Story: a history of the World in story, song and art, edited by Eva March Tappan. Each book is a compilation of selections from prose literature, poetry and pictures and offers a comprehensive presentation of the world's history, art and culture, from the early times till the beginning of the 20th century. Part IX deals with the first part of the history of England, from the early times till the reign of the Tudor kings and queens. Chapters include stories about King Arthur, William the Conqueror, Robin Hood, Queen Elizabeth and many others. - Summary by Sonia Cast list for A messenger from Rome: Cymbeline: alanmapstone / Lucius: Jim Locke / Queen: Devorah Allen / Cloten: Tomas Peter / Narrator: Sonia Cast list for Prince Arthur and the keeper Hubert: Hubert: Jim Locke / First Executioner: Devorah Allen / Arthur: Tomas Peter / Narrator: Sonia Cast list for Two scenes in the life of Henry V: Falstaff: alanmapstone / Prince Hal/King Henry V: Tomas Peter / Host: Monika M.C.r / Warwick: Jim Locke / Chief Justice: Adrian Stephens / Lancaster: Todd HW / Gloucester: SaraHale / Clarence: Sandra Schmit / Narrator: Sonia Cast list for The fall of Cardinal Wolsey: Wolsey: Jim Locke / Cromwell: alanmapstone / Narrator: Sonia

title: Narrative of Colonel Ethan Allen's Captivity
author: Ethan Allen
description: Ethan Allen's account of his military exploits and subsequent captivity, riven with drama, adventure and humor. - Summary by Steven Warner

title: Noble Woman The Life-Story of Edith Cavell
author: Ernest Protheroe
description: Edith L. Cavell (1865–1915) was a British nurse who attended to soldiers of both sides during World War I, and helped some 200 Allied soldiers escape from German-occupied Belgium, for which she was arrested, court-martialed, found guilty of treason and sentenced to death. Attempts to mount an appeal failed, and she was summarily executed within hours of the sentence by a German firing squad. Publication of the news prompted spontaneous grief and worldwide condemnation. Many memorials were created around the world, including a statue adjacent to Trafalgar Square in London. --Adapted from Wikipedia NOTE: After recording Chapter 7, the reader became aware that the subject's family pronounced the surname as it rhymes with "gravel", and he therefore pronounces it CAvel in subsequent chapters. The first edition of this book was published in 1916. The final portion of Chapter 15 is from a later edition. ( Wikipedia page on Edith Cavell)

title: The GLP-1 Journey Podcast
author: Squatch Media Group
description:
Welcome to The GLP-1 Journey Podcast

Welcome to The GLP-1 Journey Podcast, hosted by The Big Squatch—your destination for honest conversations, real experiences, and practical information about life with GLP-1 medications.Whether you're just beginning your weight-loss journey, looking for motivation, or searching for trustworthy insights, this podcast is here to support you every step of the way.

Each episode features inspiring stories, expert perspectives, and special in-studio guests who share their successes, challenges, and strategies for achieving lasting results.At The GLP-1 Journey Podcast, we believe every journey is unique, and no one should have to navigate it alone. 

Our mission is to educate, inspire, and build a community where listeners can learn, grow, and celebrate every milestone together.Your journey starts here. Welcome to The GLP-1 Journey Podcast.Disclaimer: 

The information shared on this podcast is for educational and informational purposes only and should not be considered medical advice. Always consult your healthcare provider before making decisions regarding your health, medications, or treatment.

Through conversations around branding, business, creativity, identity, leadership, and the wild process of becoming, this show explores what it really means to build something people can feel.

title: Hidden Plumbing Problems That Can Cost Homeowners Thousands
author: Christopher Lamneck
description: Many plumbing issues start small but can quickly turn into expensive repairs if they go unnoticed. In this episode, we uncover some of the most common hidden plumbing problems, including slow leaks, aging pipes, sewer line damage, and inefficient water heaters. We discuss warning signs that property owners should never ignore and explain how routine inspections can help prevent major damage. Featuring insights commonly shared by a Plumber Middlesex NJ, this conversation highlights practical ways to protect your home, reduce water waste, and avoid unexpected repair bills. Whether you own a house, manage rental properties, or operate a business, you'll learn valuable strategies for keeping your plumbing system reliable and cost-effective year-round.

author: Starr Meneely
description:
A story-telling podcast for kids and family with original songs and stories. Fall in love with new characters and songs in each episode. Each story is designed to inspire imagination and singing. 
Created and performed by Starr Meneely; composer, writer and music teacher. Starr has been working with children, writing, teaching music and telling stories, for more than thirty years. A Musical Story draws on her research on child development and her experience in music education to create engaging entertainment for children and their families.

title: Why you should avoid toxic people
author: Flora Slavins
description: Avoid toxic people because they drain your energy, disrupt your peace of mind, and often encourage negativity. Surrounding yourself with supportive, respectful, and positive people helps you grow, maintain healthy relationships, and protect your mental and emotional well-being.

title: The IRL Radio Show
author: Roberto Clavero
description: In Real Life. On this episode, Tasteless T returns from a short stint in silence, mourning his Ma. He shares stories about her from childhood, like playing with lighters and hairspray, almost burning down the family home. He brings up her homeland, Cuba and her taking him to all the C.R. Superman movies. She becomes the one constant throughout the program as he resurrects her in stories to the last day... however, in his typical fashion, he can't help but spill tea, which can make one listening pee. That and a bit more on this Episode of The IRL Radio Show with your fearless leader, Tasteless T.

title: Tom Swift and the Electronic Hydrolung (A Race Against Time)
author: Victor Appleton
description:
When a mysterious missile intercepts the US government's probe to Jupiter, Tom Swift Jr. finds himself at the center of a high-stakes underwater race. Tasked with recovering the probe from the depths of the South Atlantic, Tom quickly realizes he's not alone in this mission. A shadowy group with sinister motives is hot on his trail, determined to seize the probe for their own nefarious purposes. Kidnapping, coercion, and lethal force all come into play as Tom navigates this treacherous terrain.

In "Tom Swift and the Electronic Hydrolung," listeners will dive into a world of cutting-edge technology and relentless adventure. Tom's ingenuity shines as he employs his latest invention, the Electronic Hydrolung, to explore the ocean's depths and outsmart his competitors. With every twist and turn, the tension mounts: will Tom uncover the secrets of the probe before it's too late?

This action-packed audiobook delivers a gripping blend of science fiction and adventure, perfect for fans of thrillers and inventive storytelling. Join Tom on his perilous journey filled with unexpected allies and dangerous foes, and discover what it truly means to fight against the odds. Will he manage to recover the probe and thwart the plans of those who would use it for evil? Tune in and find out!

title: Hooper Plumbing & Air Conditioning Podcast
author: hooper-plumbing-air-conditioning
description:
The Hooper Plumbing & Air Conditioning Podcast brings you tips and advice from a family-owned company that's served Dallas and Mesquite homeowners since 1923.

Each episode covers a range of topics, including plumbing repairs, AC maintenance, water heater care, drain cleaning, and heating system maintenance. Discover how to prevent costly emergencies and maintain a comfortable home year-round.

Dealing with a clogged drain? Wondering when to replace your AC? Just want to save money on your next repair? We're here to help. Over 100 years of experience, one conversation at a time.

Hooper Plumbing & Air Conditioning. Licensed, insured, and dedicated to Dallas-Fort Worth homeowners.

title: Part of a Bigger Thing
author: Ben Swanson, Intertronics
description:
Part of a Bigger Thing is an internal Intertronics podcast that puts the spotlight on the people behind the business.

 

In each episode, Ben sits down with a member of the Intertronics team for a relaxed, long-form conversation about their life, career, experiences, interests, and the work they do today. Together, they explore the journey that brought them to Intertronics, the challenges and opportunities within their role, and the person behind the job title.

 

The aim is simple: to help us better understand each other, celebrate the people who make Intertronics what it is, and strengthen the connections that help us work together every day.

 

Whether you've worked closely with the guest for years or have only exchanged the occasional email, every episode offers a chance to get to know a colleague in a different way.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Lives of the Party: A Jalen Gonel Show
author: Jalen Gonel
description: Ever wanted to know what life is like for those around you? How someone else sees things? That's what this show is about. All my life I've been curious. I think the world we live in is an amazing place, with lots to explore. This is an outlet for my curiosity, to explore anyone and anything I find interesting. We're alive. That's worth celebrating. Hear about what's here, what's happening, in the party of life.

title: Born Before the Algorithm
author: Victory Igberase
description: Born Before the Algorithm is a podcast about people who grew up before social media, smartphones, and AI—and are now navigating a rapidly changing digital world. Through honest conversations and personal stories we explore technology, work, relationships, culture, and what it means to adapt when the future arrives faster than expected. Whether you’re keeping up or catching up, this podcast is for anyone learning to thrive in the age of algorithms.

title: Cognitive Justice
author: Stephen and Alyssa
description:
Cognitive Justice is about how mental health and the law overlap and what that means for real people. We’re Stephen and Alyssa, two students who want to figure out what really happens when these worlds meet.
We talk openly about why mental health matters in the justice system and why things don’t always feel fair. We share stories and perspectives you don’t usually hear, and we make sure our research comes from solid, peer reviewed sources so you know it’s backed by real evidence.
At the heart of it, we’re here to listen, learn, and help build a system that sees the person before the problem

title: Kant's Philosophy
author: The Voice of Reason
description: 300 years ago Immanuel Kant was born - and I want to dedicate an extensive series of lectures to his whole philosophy. I spent the past year studying the 29 volumes of Kant's collected works and reading at least a 100 pages of Kant every day, in order to now provide the probably most comprehensive and most thorough presentation on his philosophy which has been attempted so far, to show its inner systematic relations and, most of all, to identify Kant's contribution to the aufklärung of humanity.

title: The Embrace of Midnight
author: D. Renee Klassen
description: Get a glimpse into the world of the undead.  Pull back the curtain to this secretive society and discover their dark dramas, their sinister appetites, and their infuriating and petty real estate woes.  Stealing time on lonely stations while the rest of humanity sleeps, your narrator will keep you informed of all the strange, disturbing, macabre, and occasionally baffling tales that make up the other side of midnight.  Join the creatures of the night to learn their secrets, their stories, and that they may be eternal but that doesn’t mean they will ever stop bickering over literally everything.

title: Girl, Heal.
author: Shakera
description: Girl, Heal. is your Selah, a pause in a world that constantly tells women to keep going, keep performing, keep carrying. This is your invitation to slow down long enough to acknowledge who you were, embrace who you are, and affirm the woman you’re becoming. For the girl who stayed soft in hard seasons, faithful in uncertain ones, and resilient through moments she never imagined she’d survive. This is a reminder that Healed Girls, Heal Girls. There’s an entire generation waiting for you. Whole and healed.

title: fire safety equipment manufacturers
author: haseen habib
description:
Fire safety products require careful design, testing, and quality control to ensure they perform properly during emergencies. Manufacturers play an important role in developing equipment such as extinguishers, alarms, hydrant systems, protective gear, and other safety solutions that support different types of buildings and hazards.

In Karachi, industries, commercial buildings, warehouses, and residential projects often require equipment that matches local safety needs, building layouts, and operational risks. Understanding product standards, certifications, and maintenance requirements helps organizations make informed choices.

Haseen Habib Trading shares educational fire safety information to help readers understand equipment features, system requirements, and selection factors. Working with reliable fire safety equipment manufacturers helps ensure that safety products meet required specifications and support effective emergency preparedness.

title: You Saw What? Eyewitness Stories of the Unexplained
author: Jack Lee
description:
Have you ever experienced something you couldn’t explain?
'You Saw What?' is a podcast featuring real eyewitness accounts of the paranormal and unexplained. Host Jack Lee shares eerie encounters and interviews people who’ve witnessed ghosts, UFOs, Bigfoot, and other strange phenomena.
Each episode explores what was seen, felt, and how it changed those involved.
Whether you’re a believer, skeptic, or just curious, 'You Saw What?' invites you to hear these stories and decide what lies beyond the ordinary.

title: DISCIPLESHIP DECODED
author: Malden Mitchell
description:
Welcome to Discipleship Decoded — where Malden L. Mitchell breaks down the Word and keeps it a hundred. This ain't your polished, play-it-safe Christian podcast. It's real discipleship for real people — faith with the mask off.


Jesus said, "Go and make disciples of all nations." That's a command, not a suggestion. Every episode decodes what it actually looks like to follow Jesus when life is loud — real talk on discipleship, spiritual growth, biblical manhood and womanhood, prayer, purpose, relationships, and everyday Christian living. No fluff, no fake, no religion-as-performance.


 Whether you grew up in church, just found faith, or you're still figuring out what you believe, there's a seat at the table for you.

If you're into Christian podcasts, Bible teaching, and sermons that don't sugarcoat the truth, tap in and hit follow. New episodes drop every week. Unlocking the Truth. Transforming Lives.

Catch it on Apple Podcasts, Spotify, Amazon Music, iHeartRadio, and YouTube — truth over hype, every single time

From cutting accommodation costs to pulling off a getaway with friends, we're here to make travel simpler and a lot more fun. title: Center of Excellence - Pharma 4.0 by Dr. Jayant Joshi
author: Dr. Jayant Joshi
description: The Pharmaceutical Industry is undergoing a transformation driven by advancements in technology and data analytics. Smart Manufacturing, also known as Pharma 4.0, is revolutionising the way pharmaceutical products are developed, manufactured, and distributed. This podcast provides an in-depth exploration of Smart Manufacturing principles and Technologies as applied in the Pharmaceutical sector. You will gain a comprehensive understanding of how digitalization, automation, and data-driven decision-making are reshaping pharmaceutical manufacturing processes to enhance efficiency, quality, and compliance. We’ll speak with industry experts, data scientists, and supply chain leaders to uncover the opportunities and challenges that come with integrating technology into one of the world’s most critical industries. Whether you’re a Professional in Pharma, Tech, or simply curious about the future of Healthcare or Pharmaceutical Manufacturing in particular, this is the podcast for you.

title: Dead But Not Gone: An Estate Planning Podcast
author: deadbutnotgone
description: Dead but Not Gone: An Estate Planning Podcast is a podcast dedicated to demystifying estate planning for individuals and families. Hosted by knowledgeable professionals, the show provides practical advice and insights into creating effective estate plans that protect loved ones and prevent conflicts. With a focus on clarity and education, each episode addresses common questions and challenges faced by listeners, offering guidance on wills, trusts, probate, and more. Whether you're new to estate planning or looking to refine your existing plan, Dead but Not Gone: An Estate Planning Podcast equips you with the tools and knowledge to make informed decisions.

title: Sembang Songsang
author: Justice For Sisters
description:
The queers are taking back power, space, language — and everything else. And we’re doing that through this podcast.
Brought to you by Justice for Sisters and friends, Sembang Songsang features conversations with queer activists, artists, organisers and others on current affairs, culture, art and more. Every 3 weeks (or maybe 4), we will bring you discussions that centre queer perspectives and experiences in Malaysia. Through this podcast, we hope to bring more queer analysis, queer storytelling, queer lens, queer rage, and queer joy into public conversations.
Song: Queer Gaze by Shh...Diam!!!

author: Murray Russell Langton
description:
Liminal Radio - Digital is the audio home of One Create Magazine. A 25-minute radio series interviewing key figures in health, technology, creativity, hospitality, property and modern living across Thailand, the UK, Japan, the Middle East and the United States. Every episode cuts through the noise and gets to what actually matters.
Hosted by Murray Russell Langton, known as Buzz, Editor in Chief of One Create Magazine. Recorded in Bangkok, Japan, the United Kingdom, the Middle East and the United States.
New episodes weekly.

title: UniConnect
author: Uniconnect Immigration Services
description:
Welcome to the UniConnect Podcast, your trusted source for practical insights on studying, working, and immigrating to Canada. Each episode explores important topics related to Canadian immigration, including study permits, work permits, visitor visas, permanent residency, family sponsorship, and the latest immigration updates. We also share expert tips on preparing strong applications, understanding IRCC requirements, avoiding common mistakes, and choosing the right immigration pathway for your goals.
Whether you're an international student, skilled professional, or family planning a future in Canada, our podcast is designed to provide clear, reliable, and easy-to-understand information to help you navigate the immigration process with confidence. Join us as we simplify Canadian immigration and support you on every step of your journey toward new opportunities in Canada.

title: Guided Meditations with Kamara Farai
author: Kamara Farai
description:
A quiet space to slow down.
Through guided meditation, Yoga Nidra, breathwork, and somatic practice, each episode offers an opportunity to rest, regulate your nervous system, and reconnect with your body.
Created by Kamara Farai, a Los Angeles–based visual artist, meditation guide, and Yin Yoga teacher.

title: The Hidden Mechanics
author: The Pattern Lab
description:
Tired of the same everyday loops — the endless scrolling, sudden boredom, or that quiet frustration when you just can’t focus or let things go? There’s a hidden engine running it all: craving, the illusion of a fixed “me”, and old habits that keep pulling us back in.
This show gently reveals the mechanics behind ordinary human struggles and shows how simply seeing them clearly in the present moment can quietly set you free. No grand theories. Just real, relatable inquiry into ordinary life and how we can move past it.
Hidden Mechanics — real talk for anyone stuck in the same cycle.

title: Let's Over React Podcast
author: Let's Over React Crew
description:
Welcome to the Let's Over React Podcast! Join JohnGottie, PSIDEKICK, Fly, and JBoogie as they overreact to the biggest stories in sports, music, relationships, gaming, pop culture, trending news, and viral moments. Every episode is packed with hot takes, debates, laughs, and unfiltered conversations that keep you entertained and coming back for more. If it's making headlines or blowing up online, we're talking about it. 
New episodes every week. Join Overreaction Nation and let's overreact together!

title: Grace Through Growth
author: Krista Duckett
description:
Welcome to Grace, Growth & Gratitude — a faith-centered space for women with real explorations about life, motherhood, marriage, mental health, personal growth, and the everyday challenges women face.
I’m Krista, and this podcast was created to encourage women to navigate life’s highs and lows through the lens of faith, grace, and gratitude. We don’t grow despite hard things—often, God grows us through them. 
Together, we’ll talk about the hard things—overwhelm, anxiety, guilt, insecurity, relationships, resilience, and spiritual growth—while grounding ourselves in God’s truth and His promises.
This is a place to be reminded that you are seen, known, loved, and never walking alone.
Here, we believe healing happens through honesty, growth happens through surrender, and joy can still be chosen—even in hard seasons.
Come join me as we grow together, rooted in faith, growing in grace, and choosing joy.

Derm Power a dermatologist-led advocacy podcast from the California Dermatology Advocacy Network (CDAN). 
We examine the laws, scope-of-practice debates, reimbursement challenges, and healthcare policies shaping the future of physician-led dermatology and patient safety. 
Hosted by physician leaders and policy experts, each episode connects the dots between policy decisions and real-world clinical impact. 
Because protecting patient care means protecting the profession.

author: briantankconner
description: Short but informative lessons for new believers from God's Holy Word.

title: Choppin It Up Wit Chop
author: Chop
description: Choppin It Up Wit Chop is a Podcast that features real conversations about Life, Faith, Entrepreneurship, Barber culture, Family, and Success.

title: SHEROCCO
author: RKO
description:
È difficile raccontare cos’è stato Sherocco 2026.

Cinque giorni di libertà, lotta, arte, musica, incontro e cura: uno spazio per imparare, ballare, emozionarsi e immaginare.

Sherocco esiste grazie a chi, con passione e competenza, lo rende possibile.

Grazie a Sara Garbagnoli, Francesca Romana Recchia Luciani e Massimo Prearo per il coordinamento scientifico della Sherocco Academy.

Grazie a Rosi Paparella, Porpora Marcasciano e Gianmaria Colpani per il percorso laboratoriale.

Grazie a Karma B, Guide Karmiche indimenticabili del Festival, e a Nicole de Leo, voce del Festival, che ci emoziona e ispira.

Grazie al cuore di Sherocco: Giuda, Pia, Clément e Rachele — comunicazione, visual, social, foto, visite guidate e mille altre cose!

Grazie a Chiara per social e podcast, a Carola per l’assistenza artistɜ e a Sofia per l’infopoint.

Grazie a Raffaella per allestimenti e Village, e a Selene, coordinatrice e cuore dell’Academy.

Grazie a Michele, rider, montaggio, supporto logistico e morale, e ad Anita e Lara per botteghino, viaggi e spostamenti.

Grazie a Patti, Luca ed Elena per il bar.

Grazie a tuttɜ lɜ volontariɜ: Francesco, Daniela, Eleonora, Maida, Chiara, Antonella, Patrizia, Claudia, Micheal, Angel, Antonella e Greta, per la casa accogliente di Sherocco.

Grazie a Daniela Cavallo per i gadget del Festival, e a Tea e allo staff di Provinciali Service per il supporto tecnico.

Grazie a Cathy Latorre per il contributo a questa edizione.

Grazie a Ostuni Pride, al Circolo Arci Annavanna e a Luca Dell’Atti, sorella di pensiero e di cuore.

Grazie a L’Apina DJ e Annagrida DJ, a RKO, Carlo Chicco e Paola Pagone, alle librerie Un Panda sulla Luna e Spine Bookstore, alle associazioni e ai punti food Chefigolab e Rapa Rossa.

Grazie a Titti e Chicca, direzione e produzione di Sherocco, anima e core del Festival.

Grazie a voi. A chi è tornatə o ci ha scopertə, a chi ha ascoltato, studiato, danzato, discusso, marciato, cantato, lavorato, riso e condiviso con noi.

Sherocco siete voi.

Ci rivediamo il prossimo anno, con mente fertile, cuore pieno

title: Optimal
author: Clayrooksp
description: Optimal is about becoming who you were made to be, one intentional step at a time. Each episode explores practical, faith-informed ideas for growth in mind, body, and spirit — building better habits, renewing your thinking, and pursuing the fuller life God intends. From Natural Church Ministries.

title: One People - Am Echad (עם אחד)
author: Rabbi Alli Cohen
description: One People - Am Echad (עם אחד) is a podcast dedicated to bridging the gap between Israel and the Diaspora. Together, we’ll move beyond the news headlines to discover the stories and unseen realities of the people who live there!

title: The Next Step
author: Julia | Contaris Partners
description:
Every meaningful phase of growth asks the same quiet question:
What’s the next step, and am I ready for it?
The Next Step is a podcast for leaders navigating moments that matter. 
- Scaling a company
- Preparing for capital
- Modernizing operations
- Carrying responsibility that don't come with a playbook
We talk through the real work behind progress. The decisions, trade-offs, systems, and mindset shifts required when the path forward isn’t obvious.
This isn’t about overnight success.
It's about focus and taking the Next Step.

title: Packing the Word: Memorizing Scripture to Survive the Wilderness of Life
author: Packing the Word
description:
Psalm 119:11 says, "I have hidden your word in my heart, that I might not sin against you." Pack the Word in your heart like gear in your pack, so that you may survive the spiritual danger you will encounter along the narrow trail of life.
This podcast helps you memorize passages of Scripture incrementally in a "repeat after me" format. The first phrase will be repeated several times, then the next phrase will be added to the first phrase.

title: Colour Me Bad
author: Pablo and Ye
description: Conversations with a dash of controversy.

title: My MS Podcast
author: Alene Brennan
description:
Welcome to My MS Podcast! I'm Alene Brennan, your host and fellow MS sister. On this podcast I'll be sharing with you my personal experience living with this unpredictable and invisible disease, along with my professional experience as a Nutrition Coach specializing in Multiple Sclerosis.
 
This podcast is an invitation to elevate your life with MS – to start thriving with MS. If you still want to dream big, pursue your passions, or simply find peace with your diagnosis - this is for you. Here, you'll find tools, conversations, and a community dedicated to helping you to reclaim your body, mind, and life from Multiple Sclerosis.
 
Tune in every Monday, Wednesday and Friday and be sure to hit that subscribe button so you never miss an episode.

title: Geek Speak
author: Joey & Christian
description: Tech Talk

title: The Hidden Forces That Shape Wealth
author: morris
description: Money is more than income, budgets, and investments. Money Beyond Numbers explores the invisible psychology, forgotten financial history, hidden market mechanics, and rare wealth-building principles that influence every financial decision we make. Every episode uncovers unusual stories, timeless lessons, and practical insights designed to help listeners think differently about money—without hype, unrealistic promises, or financial jargon.

title: Imperial China Chronicles
author: Imperial China Chronicles
description: Welcome to Imperial China Chronicles, your ultimate destination for exploring the 5,000-year history of the Middle Kingdom. We dive deep into the rise and fall of powerful dynasties, from the legendary Three Kingdoms to the Forbidden City's deepest secrets. Our documentary-style videos uncover the strategies of ancient warlords, the lives of controversial emperors, and the cultural inventions that shaped the world.

title: Factory to Future
author: Zhipeng (Brian) LUO
description:
Factory to Future explores what it really takes to build technology inside traditional industries. These are stories about building products, teams, AI systems, and a life worth living — shared as they happen.
Each episode shares real stories about building products, teams, AI systems, and a meaningful career—from factory floors to the future of intelligent manufacturing.
Whether you’re an engineer, product builder, founder, or simply curious about how technology changes industries, you’ll find practical lessons learned in real time.

title: Making the Call
author: Ulla Hansen
description: Discussing tennis' hottest stories. But let's make it nuanced.

title: Tindo Solar - Battery Rebate
author: Zak
description: Australian-Made Solar with Expert Battery Rebate Support

title: BassMus FM
author: Bunyodbek Zokirov
description: Bass music dunyosi

title: Style talk with Kevin Riddle the most stylish teenager in America
author: Kevin Riddle live
description: a podcast that helps teenage and adult men dress like the best versions of themselves

title: B2B Pest Control Marketing
author: MD ROKON BISWAS
description:
B2B Pest Control Marketing Podcast – A podcast dedicated to helping pest control business owners and operators grow their companies through proven marketing strategies, SEO, lead generation, branding, sales, customer retention, and business development insights.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Sunny's Silver Linings
author: Sunny Kaila
description: Sunny's Silver Linings is a Top MSP Channel Podcast hosted by the CEO of IT By Design, Sunny Kaila. Sunny and his guests cover a wide range of MSP and Talent-based topics, including MSP services, cybersecurity, customer service, and strategies to achieve growth and success in the channel. Listen now to take your business to the next level!  https://hubs.ly/Q02DrxnQ0

title: macRO Essays
author: macRO Essays
description: Trying to make it make sense.

title: Earth Update: Climate News, in Short
author: Laurie Parsons and Ilma Bogdan
description: Earth Update is the new podcast mini-series from the Royal Geographical Society's Climate Change Research Group. In just ten short minutes, we will bring you a breakdown of one of the world's most pressing environmental topics, in partnership with a world leading expert.

title: Research to Reality in Rare Syndromes
author: Cerebra Network
description: The Cerebra Network are excited to launch Research to Reality in Rare Syndromes. This new podcast series brings researchers, professionals and parent carers together to discuss the challenges, evidence, and practical solutions that can make a real difference to families' lives.

title: The Merging Path Podcast
author: Brooks E. Scott
description: A podcast to create bold and inspiring relationships at work and at home, the Merging path Podcast is hosted by professionally trained and certified Executive Coach Brooks E. Scott from Merging Path Coaching. It explores a range of leadership, management and interpersonal communications concepts to help us all have conversations we continue to avoid.

title: Between You & B
author: Brooklynn Hildre
description: Real people, real talk, no script. Goals, hard lessons, and a little tea on the side. Pull up a chair. I’m Brooklynn — creator, creative, entrepreneur, daughter, friend, all of it, all the time. I’ve come across, and I’m lucky to know, so many people with stories and knowledge I wish I could encapsulate and watch back for reference — and now I can.

title: Jablam Sports Hockey
author: Peter Bojarinov
description: Your source for NHL news, trade analysis, free agency, rumors, and in-depth discussions on the biggest stories across the league.

title: Overset
author: Oscar Haumann & Marcus Feldthus
description: En podcast om alt det vi oversér, når vi tror, at større altid er bedre. En uformel samtale mellem Oscar Haumann, og Marcus Feldthus, der sammen står bag House of Small. Hver gang tager vi fat i noget, der sker ude i verden, noget vi selv er stødt på i vores egen forretning, og et par spørgsmål fra jer lyttere. Alt sammen set gennem det, vi kalder smådriftsfordele.

title: soulsinsyncpodcast
author: Stacy and Vicky
description:
After singing together in middle school and high school and losing touch for 40 years, Stacy and Vicky reconnect through authentic conversation, music, friendship and energy healing. 
Stacy is a well-known backup singer who toured with some of the most iconic artists of our time, including Cher, Tina Turner, Joe Cocker, Janet Jackson and Pink. Now, she shares her passion for music in music therapy. 
Vicky was a television writer/producer, working in NYC for networks including NBC, Nickelodeon and Comedy Central. She is a Reiki Master Teacher, helping clients with energy healing. 
Join us!

title: Unwrapped With Gift
author: John Ashi Gift
description: Unwrapped with Gift is a podcast where we explore life's realities through honest conversations about growth, health, business, relationships, struggles, addiction, and personal development. One conversation at a time, we uncover lessons that inspire, heal, and transform.

title: Atmalok
author: Prabhu Shree
description: Vasudev Sarvam

title: No Effort
author: Roach and Atomic
description: Hosted by Roach and Atomic who try to make each other laugh through personal stories, current events, debates and more.

title: Max Effort Audio Network
author: Cory Gregory
description: The Max Effort Audio Network brings together a collection of personal development shows and snippets to help people get through their daily workouts & life. Choose walking lunges as your daily conditioning for maximum effectiveness. Feed your body and brain at the same time, stay motivated, and take your game to an entirely different level. Welcome to the crazy.

title: Anatomy of a Myth
author: Teertha Kattamuri
description:
Just because a health trend goes viral doesn't mean it's backed by science.
We are surrounded by health advice, internet diagnoses, and wellness trends that sound true but have zero scientific backing. Anatomy of a Myth is dedicated to separating fact from fiction, acting as your reliable guide through the noise of health misinformation. From historical misconceptions that just won't die to modern-day internet health fads, we break down complex science into fascinating, easy-to-understand episodes.

title: The Strategy Gap
author: Pete
description: There's a gap between strategy formulation and execution. The Strategy Gap podcast is short concise episodes of around 10-15 mins with tips and actions to help ambitious SME's nail their goals and execute their plans better

title: Educating Humans
author: Difff & James
description: Discussions about Classical and Liberal Arts education in Primary and Secondary schools in Australia. What is it, how it might be achieved, and most importantly, why it matters.

title: Joe Cotton's Stock Picks- Weekly Stock Picks from A Champion Stock Picker
author: Joseph Cotton
description: Joe Cotton provides a "Stock of the Week" and reviews his latest Market Letter or discusses current Market conditions, or various stocks..

title: Foxic
author: Gracie Fox
description: Welcome to Foxic. Hosted by Gracie Fox, where no topic is off limits. Featuring real conversations with comedians, DJs, producers, fitness professionals, flight attendants, reality TV personalties, and everything in between. From comedy and music to fitness and pop culture, expect laughs, stories, behind-the-scenes moments, and the occasional hot take.

title: The Little Things Podcast
author: Katie Clarke
description: I'm Katie, a girl in her 20s who believes the little things in life make the big things happen. They also bring the most joy and inspiration! You can expect me to be occasionally joined by friends as I chat candidly about navigating college, wellness & routines, relationships, and life.

title: My Unlikely Career
author: Unlikely Careers
description: Celebrating people who have carved out unique career paths and found success on their own terms. Whether you're just starting your career, stuck at a crossroads, or ready to make a change, this podcast for you. Join us for intimate conversations about tough choices, unexpected turns, and the power of listening to your inner voice.

title: The Brave Man Project with Hamish Niven
author: Hamish Niven
description:
This is not a podcast about fixing yourself.
The Brave Man Project is a sanctuary for the modern man caught between the suffocating mask of performance and the silent weight of unaddressed trauma. A dual-lens journey. Mirrors and windows.
The Mirror reflects the lie you've been telling yourself. "I'm alright, mate." Men who were Captain of the Universe, who wore the mask so long they forgot their own face, who loaded bricks onto their backs until the weight became lethal. Then they fell. And they chose to look.
The Window expands your vision. Men who woke up and saw the world in Technicolor. Men who lead others through blue doors. They do not reflect your collapse. They prove you were never broken to begin with.
In this teaser, you'll hear fragments of what's coming. The unvarnished truth about what it actually took.
This is for the man tired of the noise and the bullshit. The man exhausted from carrying a mask. Whether you're currently being kicked by the universe or simply tired of the black-and-white grind.

title: The GMW Podcast
author: Garcia Media Works
description:
The GMW Podcast delivers hard truths about social media for business owners who are tired of being burned by bad agencies, wasting time doing it themselves, and not getting results. Real strategy, no sugarcoating, from the agency behind 150 million organic views. 


Want to learn more? Visit garciamediaworks.com

title: Certifying the Future™
author: Scott D'Amico
description: Certifying the Future is where emerging technology meets workforce reality. Hosted by Scott D’Amico and presented by CertNexus, the podcast brings together subject matter experts, training leaders, credentialing voices, and industry stakeholders to explore how AI, cybersecurity, data, and other emerging technologies are changing the way we work. Each episode looks at the implications for skills, certifications, and career readiness, with practical insights for both employers and employees.

title: About a Week
author: About a Week
description:
Welcome to About A Week, the weekly comedy and news podcast hosted by Alex and Lewis.
 
Every week, these two long-time mates bridge the digital divide to break down the biggest headlines, the absolute chaos of the internet, and the bizarre stories that made them say, "Wait, what?" From major global news to personal studio updates, and questionable banter, it’s your definitive—and completely unofficial—seven-day debrief.
 
Expect recurring segments and comedy, alongside deep dives into UK Politics, Sports, Celebrity Gossip.
Hit subscribe to find out exactly what went down in... About a Week.

title: The TNRambler Podcast
author: TNRamblerPod
description: Politics, Culture Movie, and anything else I have on my mind.

title: Paroles de patients
author: Ramsay Santé
description: La Direction Recherche et Enseignement soutient 280 projets de recherche tous centrés autour d’un objectif : améliorer la prise en charge des patients. Dans cette saison, nous vous emmenons à leur rencontre pour comprendre comment la recherche change la vie des patients et des médecins du groupe Ramsay.

title: The Engineers Next Door
author: Engineers Australia WA
description:
This podcast is about turning the complexity of the energy transition into plain language, real conversations, and ultimately, a practical plan for Western Australia. We’ll bring in guests with different—and sometimes competing—views, because better plans come from robust discussion, not talking points.

Less talk. More planning. This is about how the transition will get done—so we can get on and build it.

title: Cleats y Cultura
author: Cleats y Cultura
description: Cleats y Cultura is a podcast celebrating the connection between sports and Latino culture. We talk baseball beyond the superstars, highlighting the overlooked players, underdogs, and Latino athletes whose stories deserve recognition.
